---
title: "Shell Node T Demo"
format: html
filters:
  - tlang
---

# Introduction

This demo showcases a T pipeline incorporating **Shell** nodes (via `shn`) using tools like `awk` for data processing.

# AWK Processing Results

The `awk_node` calculates summary statistics directly from the raw data using a shell command.

```{t}
#| label: awk-results
node_awk = read_node("awk_node")
df_awk = read_csv(node_awk.path)
df_awk
```

# T Language Integration

The `final_summary` node then consumes the AWK output using a CSV deserializer.

```{t}
#| label: final-summary
node_final = read_node("final_summary")
val_final = t_read_json(node_final.path)
val_final
```

# Conclusion

The T language makes it easy to incorporate legacy scripts or specialized command-line tools into a modern data pipeline.
